caption:
📍Koffee Mameya Kakeru, Tokyo | #koffeemameyakakeru

We left @koffee_mameya_kakeru feeling like we just watched a spectacular concert 🤩

Koffee Mameya Kakeru leans completely into its philosophy of ‘kakeru’ which means ‘to multiply’ or the concept of various elements coming together to create something more significant and valuable than each individual part.

Focusing on the Japanese concept of ‘kodawari’ - aspiration to perfection and attention to detail, the master brewers at Koffee Mameya Kakeru focus on stretching coffee’s potential and delivering the best cup possible. 

Tasting courses start at ¥3500, which comes with 3 drinks brewed using different methods, accompanied by complementing sweets. Premium coffee course and cocktail coffee course are available too! 

Note: The banana au-lait in the video was an a-la-carte order

Reservations are a MUST and is available on their page

For more perks and curated gems, 
★ linktr.ee/shoyutheworld

#japancafe #japancoffee #tokyocoffee #tokyocafe #tokyo
transcript: That's it, coffee will never be the same again. Coffee Mamiya Kakeru is anything but just a cafe. Imagine a chemistry lab but on a stage. You're sitting front and centre watching an orchestra of baristas performing their craft like a symphony. For $35 per person, we got an omakase course but for coffee. Using seasonal beans curated from around the world, this maestro served us a three-part symphony. Each brew came with sweets pairing and the experience was like a musical progression. We never knew coffee could taste like this. The baristas are also really chill. They took the time to give interesting insights on our drinks. They also speak English so don't worry if you're not comfortable in Japanese. The course ended with this classic filtered brewed coffee. I don't think I have to tell you how blissful I was. But the real finale was this banana ole. No kidding, it felt like I was teleported to a cocktail lounge without leaving my seat. Share this with a coffee travel buddy.
